zh.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter於1436年出生在英國越可爾。他和Elizabeth Thornton至少有3個女兒。他去世於1507年11月13日在英國達勒姆盧姆利享年71歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter于1436年出生在英国越可爾。他和Elizabeth Thornton至少有3个女儿。他去世于1507年11月13日在英国達勒姆盧姆利享年71岁。

nameMeanings.name: Carter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a transporter of goods, from Middle English carter(e) ‘carter’ (Anglo-Norman French car(e)tier, Old French charetier, medieval Latin carettarius, carettator). The Old French word coalesced with the earlier Middle English word cart(e) ‘cart’, which is from either Old Norse kartr or Old English cræt, both of which, like the Late Latin word, were probably derived from Celtic. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: shortened form of   McCarter  .
Americanized form of German   Karter   ‘carder’.

variants.name: Charman
meanings: English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for a carter, carrier, or coachman, from Middle English (Old French) char, car ‘car, cart, carriage’ + Middle English man ‘man’. Compare Carter and Carman 2.

English (Sussex and Surrey): occupational name for someone who did odd jobs, from Middle English char(re), chere (Old English cier(r), cer(r), cear(r), cær(r)) ‘turn (of activity), small job, chore’ + man. Compare Carman 2.
In some cases probably also an Americanized form of Slovenian Čarman (see Carman 5).

variants.name: Chartier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Adelard, Alcide, Emile, Gilles, Raoul, Rosaire, Alphonse.
French: shortened form of charretier, an occupational name for a carter, from an agent derivative of Old French charette ‘cart’.
French Canadian: altered form of English   Carter  , a cognate of 1 above. Compare   Sharkey   and   Shorkey  .

variants.name: Karter
meanings: German: occupational name for a carder (someone who prepares wool for spinning), from Middle High German karter. Compare Carter .

variants.name: McCarter
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   McArthur  .

variants.name: Carrier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Cecile, Alain, Gaetan, Gilles, Jacques, Laurent, Normand.

English and southern French: from Middle English, Old French car(r)ier (from Late Latin carrarius, a derivative of carrum ‘cart, wagon’, a word of Gaulish origin), an occupational name for a carter (see Carter ), in French also for a cartwright. Compare French Charrier .
French: occupational name for a stonemason or quarryman, carrier. Compare Coyer .

variants.name: Cartman
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): occupational name from Middle English cartman ‘man who operated a cart for the transport of goods’. Compare Carter .
Americanized form of German Kartmann (see Kartman ).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eudard

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter was born in 1436, in Yorkshire, England. He had at least 3 daughters with Elizabeth Thornton. He died on 13 November 1507, in Lumley, Durham, England, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter est né en 1436, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 3 filles avec Elizabeth Thornton. Il est décédé le 13 novembre 1507, à Lumley, Durham, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter wurde 1436 in Yorkshire, England geboren. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Thornton mindestens 3 Töchter. Er starb am 13. November 1507 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Lumley, Durham, England.

it.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter è nato il nel 1436 nel Yorkshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 3 figlie con Elizabeth Thornton. Morì il 13 novembre 1507, nel Lumley, Durham, England, all'età di 71 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Edward Carterは1436年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた。彼はElizabeth Thorntonとの間に少なくとも3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1507年11月13日にイギリスダルハムルムリーで71歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter(은)는 1436년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났다. 그는 Elizabeth Thornton(와)과 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1507년 11월 13일 영국 뒤람 럼리에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter nasceu em 1436, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhas com Elizabeth Thornton. Ele faleceu em 13 de novembro de 1507, em Lumley, Durham, Inglaterra, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter родился в 1436 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У него было по крайней мере 3 дочери от Elizabeth Thornton. Он умер 13 ноября 1507 г. в местности лумли, дурам, Англия в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: John Edward Carter nació en 1436, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijas con Elizabeth Thornton. Murió el 13 de noviembre de 1507, en Lumley, Durham, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años.
